clearDocuments.tooltip: Remove all documents from the knowledge base

clearDocuments.description: This will remove all documents and their extracted knowledge from the system

clearDocuments.confirm: Are you sure you want to remove all documents?
clearDocuments.clearCache: Also clear the AI response cache

deleteDocuments.title: Delete Selected Documents
deleteDocuments.description: This will permanently delete the selected documents and their extracted knowledge
deleteDocuments.warning: WARNING: Deleted documents cannot be recovered!
deleteDocuments.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete {{count}} selected document(s)?
deleteDocuments.deleteFileOption: Also delete the original uploaded files
deleteDocuments.deleteFileTooltip: Check this to also remove the original files stored on the server
deleteDocuments.deleteLLMCacheOption: Also clear the AI cache for these documents
deleteDocuments.busy: The system is busy — please try again in a moment
deleteDocuments.notAllowed: You do not have permission to perform this action

retrieval.startPrompt: Type your question below to search your documents
retrieval.placeholder: Ask a question about your documents...
retrieval.error: Something went wrong — please try again

querySettings.parametersDescription: Adjust how your questions are answered
querySettings.queryModeTooltip:
Choose how DocForge searches your documents:
• Basic Search: Fast text matching across passages
• Focused Search: Finds specific people, organisations and topics
• Big Picture: Looks for themes and broad patterns
• Balanced: Combines focused and broad search
• Full Search: Uses all available methods (recommended)
• Direct to AI: Skips document search — asks the AI directly
querySettings.responseFormatTooltip:
Choose how the answer is structured:
• Multiple paragraphs: detailed, flowing text
• Single paragraph: concise summary
• Bullet points: easy-to-scan list
querySettings.topKTooltip: How many topics and connections from the knowledge map to include when answering. Higher = more context, but slower. Default: 60.
querySettings.chunkTopKTooltip: How many text passages from your documents to read when answering. Higher = more thorough, but slower. Default: 20.
querySettings.maxEntityTokensTooltip: How much space to give topic information in the answer context. Increase if answers about specific entities feel incomplete.
querySettings.maxRelationTokensTooltip: How much space to give relationship information in the answer context. Increase if answers about how things relate feel incomplete.
querySettings.maxTotalTokensTooltip: Overall limit on how much information is included when generating an answer. Higher values allow longer, more detailed answers.
querySettings.historyTurnsTooltip: How many previous questions and answers to remember when answering your next question. Set to 0 to treat each question independently.
querySettings.onlyNeedContextTooltip: Returns only the relevant source passages without generating a written answer. Useful for reviewing the raw evidence.
querySettings.onlyNeedPromptTooltip: Returns the internal query sent to the AI instead of the answer. Technical option for debugging.
querySettings.streamResponseTooltip: Show the answer as it is being written, word by word. Turn off if you prefer to see the complete answer all at once.
querySettings.userPromptTooltip: Add a persistent instruction that applies to every answer — for example: 'Answer in Romanian' or 'Keep answers under 150 words'.
querySettings.userPromptPlaceholder: e.g. Answer in Romanian. Keep answers concise.
querySettings.enableRerankTooltip: Reorders retrieved passages by relevance before generating an answer, improving accuracy. Recommended when available.

apiKeyAlert.title: API Key Required
apiKeyAlert.description: Enter your API key to access the service

settingsPanel.title: System Info
settingsPanel.description: View the current DocForge server configuration
settingsPanel.editNote: These settings are configured by your system administrator via environment variables. Restart the server after making changes.

chunking.title: Text Splitting
chunking.description: Controls how documents are divided into searchable passages
chunking.methodTooltip:
How documents are divided into passages:
• Token Size: fixed-length chunks
• Semantic: splits at natural meaning boundaries
• Hybrid: semantic splitting with a size safety net
chunking.chunkSizeTooltip: Maximum length of each passage (measured in words/tokens). Larger passages capture more context but cost more to index.
chunking.chunkOverlapTooltip: How many words are shared between adjacent passages. A small overlap helps questions that span a passage boundary.
chunking.semanticThresholdTooltip: Controls where the AI splits text on meaning shifts. Higher values create fewer, longer passages; lower values create more, shorter ones.
chunking.semanticMinSizeTooltip: Passages shorter than this will be merged with the next one. Prevents tiny fragments.
chunking.semanticMaxTokensTooltip: If a semantic passage grows beyond this limit, it falls back to fixed-size splitting.

query.title: Search Defaults
query.description: Default values used when answering questions
query.topKTooltip: Default number of knowledge map topics included in answers
query.chunkTopKTooltip: Default number of text passages read when answering
query.maxEntityTokensTooltip: Default space allocated for topic information
query.maxRelationTokensTooltip: Default space allocated for relationship information
query.maxTotalTokensTooltip: Default total information budget for generating answers
query.historyTurnsTooltip: Default number of previous exchanges remembered

rerank.title: Smart Ranking
rerank.description: Improves answer quality by re-ordering retrieved passages by relevance
rerank.minScoreTooltip: Passages scoring below this threshold are excluded from the answer. Range: 0–1.

summary.title: Summaries
summary.description: Controls how topic and connection summaries are generated
summary.contextSizeTooltip: How much text the AI reads when creating a summary

docProcessingPanel.title: Document Processing
docProcessingPanel.description: Configure how documents are read, how images are described, and how text is divided into passages.
docProcessingPanel.infoNote: Changes take effect immediately for new uploads but are reset when the server restarts. For permanent changes, update the .env configuration file.

engine.title: Reading Engine
engine.description: Choose how documents are read. The advanced engine (Docling) preserves tables, figures, and document structure.
engine.engineDesc: Basic: text-only extraction. Advanced (Docling): preserves tables, figures, and layout structure.
engine.imagesScaleDesc: Resolution scale for extracted images. Higher values give better quality but larger files. 1.0 = standard, 2.0 = high resolution.

vision.title: Image Analysis
vision.description: When enabled, charts and figures are analysed by an AI vision model and their content is included in search results.
vision.enabledDesc: Requires the advanced reading engine. Each image is sent to the AI for a text description.
vision.willDescribe: Images will be analysed and described
vision.modelDesc: The AI model used to analyse images (e.g. google/gemini-2.0-flash-001)
vision.baseUrlDesc: The address of the AI service (e.g. OpenRouter, Azure)
vision.maxFiguresDesc: Limits how many images are analysed per document to control costs.
vision.promptDesc: The instruction sent to the AI for each image. Describes what information to extract.
vision.apiKeyNote: The API key for image analysis is configured by the server administrator and cannot be changed from this interface.

chunking.title: Passage Splitting
chunking.description: Controls how extracted text is divided into passages for indexing and search.
chunking.methodDesc: Basic: splits at a fixed word count. Smart (Semantic): splits where meaning changes. Hybrid: smart splitting with a size safety net.
chunking.chunkSizeDesc: Maximum length of each passage. Longer passages capture more context but are slower to index.
chunking.overlapDesc: Words shared between consecutive passages. A small overlap helps when a question spans two passages.
chunking.semanticThresholdDesc: How sensitive the AI is to meaning changes when splitting. Higher = fewer, longer passages.
chunking.semanticMinSizeDesc: Passages shorter than this are merged with the next. Prevents tiny fragments.
chunking.semanticMaxTokensDesc: If a smart passage grows beyond this limit, fixed-size splitting is used as a fallback.
chunking.note: Basic splitting divides text at a fixed word count. Smart splitting detects natural meaning boundaries. Hybrid uses smart splitting but falls back to fixed-size for very long passages.

extraction.title: Knowledge Extraction
extraction.description: Controls how topics and connections are identified in your documents. Configure via the .env file.

evaluationPanel.title: Answer Quality Check
evaluationPanel.description: Test how accurately and relevantly DocForge answers questions from your documents
evaluationPanel.runError: Could not start the quality check
evaluationPanel.runTooltip: Run a set of test questions to evaluate answer quality
evaluationPanel.ragasNotInstalled: The quality check feature is not installed. Please contact your system administrator.
evaluationPanel.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this result?
evaluationPanel.noResultsDescription: Run a quality check to see how well DocForge answers questions from your documents.
evaluationPanel.selectDatasetDescription: Select a set of test questions to evaluate answer quality

about.title: About DocForge
about.subtitle: Intelligent document analysis — ask questions, get cited answers
about.description: DocForge reads your documents (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, Markdown, and more) and builds a searchable knowledge base. Using AI with visual understanding, it extracts text, tables, charts, and figures — even from scanned documents. It maps topics and their connections so you can ask questions in plain language and receive accurate answers that cite the original source.

features.multimodalDesc: Extracts content from PDFs, Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and plain text — including tables, figures, and equations from scanned or complex documents.
features.knowledgeGraphDesc: Automatically identifies topics, people, organisations, and their relationships to create an interactive map you can explore visually.
features.taxonomyDesc: Configured to recognise the specific topics that matter for your documents — improving the accuracy of both search and answers.
features.hybridRetrievalDesc: Searches your documents using several methods simultaneously — direct text matching, topic-based search, and relationship traversal — for the most accurate answers.
features.evaluationDesc: Run automated tests to measure how accurate, relevant, and complete DocForge's answers are.

taxonomy.title: Topic Types
taxonomy.description: DocForge recognises specific types of topics in your documents. This list is configured for your domain — it tells the AI which concepts to look for when building the knowledge map.
taxonomy.defaultDesc: This installation is configured for EU structural fund evaluation reports and Romanian policy documents.
taxonomy.domainTitle: EU Cohesion Policy Topic Types (16 domain-specific)
taxonomy.domainDesc: These topic types are specifically designed for EU Structural Fund analysis: operational programmes, managing authorities, budgets, regulations, and regions.
taxonomy.extensible: Topic types can be changed in the Settings tab. Changing them requires re-processing your documents to rebuild the knowledge map.

about.chatPrefixDesc: Type a /shortcut before your question to temporarily change the search method for that message only.

chatPrefixes.local: Focus on specific named topics — best for precise who/what questions.
chatPrefixes.global: Look for broad themes — best for overview questions.
chatPrefixes.naive: Direct text search without the knowledge map — fastest.
chatPrefixes.mix: Use all search methods together — recommended for best results.
chatPrefixes.bypass: Skip document search and ask the AI directly.

about.customPromptDesc: The 'Extra Instructions' field in Search Settings adds a standing instruction to every answer. Here are some useful examples:

customPrompts.label: Keep it short
customPrompts.value: Keep the answer under 150 words. No bullet points.

customPrompts.label: Expert tone
customPrompts.value: Answer as a senior EU structural funds evaluator with formal, technical language.

customPrompts.label: Include numbers
customPrompts.value: Always include specific figures and percentages when available.

customPrompts.label: Cite sources inline
customPrompts.value: Cite the source document after each claim, e.g. (POCU Report, p.12).

customPrompts.label: End with a summary
customPrompts.value: End every answer with a one-sentence summary in bold.

tabs.documents: Upload and manage your documents (PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, and more). See what has been processed and what is still pending.
tabs.docProcessing: Configure how documents are read, how images are described, and how text is divided into searchable passages.
tabs.knowledgeGraph: Explore the interactive map of topics and connections extracted from your documents.
tabs.retrieval: Ask questions in plain language and get answers with citations from your documents.
tabs.evaluation: Run automated quality checks to measure how accurately DocForge answers your test questions.
tabs.api: Technical API documentation for developers who need programmatic access to DocForge.
tabs.settings: View the server configuration: AI models, storage, and ranking settings.
